/-!
# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial random distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s ⊆ u) :

This defines what it means for a random variable `X` to be `p`-Bernoulli on a set `u` with respect to a probability measure `P`: it means that `X` has the distribution `Ber(u, p)`.

The key lemma is:

  hasLaw_congr hXY

This follows directly from the fact that having a certain distribution is preserved under equality in probability.

/-!
# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial random distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

variable (u p) in
-- TODO: Generalise to countable `ι` and finite `u`. See the TODO on `infinitePi_pi`.
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s ⊆ u) :

    Ber(u, p) {s}
    _ = ∏ i, ((if i ∈ u ↔ i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else 0) +
          if i ∈ s then 0 else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      simp [bernoulliOn_apply, Set.image_singleton, Set.indicator]
      simp [ENNReal.smul_def]
    _ = ∏ i ∈ u, (if i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      rw [← Finset.prod_subset u.subset_univ (by
        simpa +contextual [ite_add_ite, ← ENNReal.coe_add] using fun _ ↦ mt (@hsu _))]
      simp +contextual [ite_add_ite]
    _ = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (↑u \ s).ncard := by

        Set.inter_eq_right.2 hsu, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.diff_eq_compl_inter, Set.inter_comm]

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

variable (u p) in
-- TODO: Generalise to countable `ι` and finite `u`. See the TODO on `infinitePi_pi`.
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s ⊆ u) :
    Ber(u, p) {s} = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (u \ s).ncard := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype ι
  lift u to Finset ι using Set.toFinite _
  calc
    Ber(u, p) {s}
    _ = ∏ i, ((if i ∈ u ↔ i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else 0) +
          if i ∈ s then 0 else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      simp [bernoulliOn_apply, Set.image_singleton, Set.indicator]
      simp [ENNReal.smul_def]
    _ = ∏ i ∈ u, (if i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      rw [← Finset.prod_subset u.subset_univ (by
        simpa +contextual [ite_add_ite, ← ENNReal.coe_add] using fun _ ↦ mt (@hsu _))]
      simp +contextual [ite_add_ite]
    _ = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (↑u \ s).ncard := by
      simp [Finset.prod_ite, ← Set.ncard_coe_finset, Set.setOf_and,
        Set.inter_eq_right.2 hsu, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.diff_eq_compl_inter, Set.inter_comm]

/-- A random variable `X : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is

lemma isBernoulliOn_congr (hXY : X =ᵐ[P] Y) : IsBernoulliOn X u p P ↔ IsBernoulliOn Y u p P :=

To show this, we need to show that for any measurable set `A` in `Set ι`, the probability that `fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω` is in `A` is equal to the probability that `fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω` is in `A`.

Let's think about how to approach this. The key insight is that `X` is `p`-Bernoulli on `u`, so the distribution of `X` is `Ber(u, p)`.
This means that for any `ω`, the value `X ω` is a subset of `u` such that each element `k ∈ u` is included in `X ω` with probability `p` independently of the others.

Therefore, for any `ω`, the membership of `i` in `X ω` and the membership of `j` in `X ω` are independent Bernoulli trials with success probability `p` each, since `i, j ∈ u`.

The proof should proceed as follows:

1. Use the definition of `IsBernoulliOn` which is `HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P`.
2. The definition of `HasLaw` implies that `P` is the distribution of `X`. So `map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) (law X)`.
3. We need to show that `map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) (law X) = map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) (law X)`.
4. This is equivalent to showing that `fun ω => i ∈ X ω` and `fun ω => j ∈ X ω` are identically distributed with respect to the law of `X`.
5. By the definition of identically distributed, we need to show that for any measurable set `A`, `P(i ∈ X ∈ A) = P(j ∈ X ∈ A)`.
6. Since `X` is `p`-Bernoulli on `u`, the distribution of `X` is `Ber(u, p)`.
7. The definition of `Ber(u, p)` implies that for any `ω`, `i ∈ X ω` and `j ∈ X ω` are independent Bernoulli trials with parameter `p`.
8. Therefore, `P(i ∈ X) = p` and `P(j ∈ X) = p`.
9. The events `i ∈ X` and `j ∈ X` are independent because of the structure of the product measure `Ber(u, p)`.
10. Therefore, `P(i ∈ X ∈ A) = P(j ∈ X ∈ A)` for any measurable `A`.

/-!
# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

variable (u p) in
-- TODO: Generalise to countable `ι` and finite `u`. See the TODO on `infinitePi_pi`.
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s ⊆ u) :
    Ber(u, p) {s} = toNNReal p ^ s

Bernoulli on `u`, `P` is the product measure `infinitePi (fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)`. 

Let's compute `P(i ∈ X ω)`. The event `i ∈ X ω` is equivalent to `i ∈ u` (since `X ω` is a subset of `u` with probability 1). So `P(i ∈ X ω) = P(i ∈ u) = p`. Similarly, `P(j ∈ X ω) = P(j ∈ u) = p`.

Now, for the general case: `P(i ∈ X ω ∈ A)`. This is `P(i ∈ X ω ∧ (i ∈ X ω → (j ∈ X ω ∈ A)))`. Since `i ∈ X ω` is equivalent to `i ∈ u`, and `j ∈ X ω` is equivalent to `j ∈ u`, we need `P(i ∈ u ∧ (i ∈ u → (j ∈ u ∈ A)))`. 

But `i ∈ u` is true (given by `hi`), so we need `P(j ∈ u ∈ A)`. This is `P(j ∈ u ∧ (j ∈ u → A))`. Since `j ∈ u` is true (given by `hj`), we need `P(A)`. So `P(i ∈ X ω ∈ A) = P(A)`.

Similarly, `P(j ∈ X ω ∈ A) = P(A)`. So they are equal.

    Ber(u, p) {s} = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (u \ s).ncard := by

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

variable (u p) in
-- TODO: Generalise to countable `ι` and finite `u`. See the TODO on `infinitePi_pi`.
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s ⊆ u) :
    Ber(u, p) {s} = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (u \ s).ncard := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype ι
  lift u to Finset ι using Set.toFinite _
  calc
    Ber(u, p) {s}
    _ = ∏ i, ((if i ∈ u ↔ i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else 0) +
          if i ∈ s then 0 else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      simp [bernoulliOn_apply, Set.image_singleton, Set.indicator]
      simp [ENNReal.smul_def]
    _ = ∏ i ∈ u, (if i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      rw [← Finset.prod_subset u.subset_univ (by
        simpa +contextual [ite_add_ite, ← ENNReal.coe_add] using fun _ ↦ mt (@hsu _))]
      simp +contextual [ite_add_ite]
    _ = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (↑u \ s).ncard := by
      simp [Finset.prod_ite, ← Set.ncard_coe_finset, Set.setOf_and,
        Set.inter_eq_right.2 hsu, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.diff_eq_compl_inter, Set.inter_comm]

/-!
# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial random distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

variable (u p) in
-- TODO: Generalise to countable `ι` and finite `u`. See the TODO on `infinitePi_pi`.
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s ⊆ u) :
    Ber(u, p) {s} = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (u \ s).ncard := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype ι
  lift u to Finset ι using Set.toFinite _
  calc
    Ber(u, p) {s}
    _ = ∏ i, ((if i ∈ u ↔ i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else 0) +
          if i ∈ s then 0 else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      simp [bernoulliOn_apply, Set.image_singleton, Set.indicator]
      simp [ENNReal.smul_def]
    _ = ∏ i ∈ u, (if i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      rw [← Finset.prod_subset u.subset_univ (by
        simpa +contextual [ite_add_ite, ← ENNReal.coe_add] using fun _ ↦ mt (@hsu _))]
      simp +contextual [ite_add_ite]
    _ = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (↑u \ s).ncard := by
      simp [Finset.prod_ite, ← Set.ncard_coe_finset, Set.setOf_and,
        Set.inter_eq_right.2 hsu, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.diff_eq_compl_inter, Set.inter_comm]

/-! ### Bernoulli random variables -/

variable (X u p P) in
/-- A random variable `X : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBernoulliOn : Prop := HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P

lemma isBernoulliOn_congr (hXY : X =ᵐ[P] Y) : IsBernoulliOn X u p P ↔ IsBernoulliOn Y u p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hXY

lemma IsBernoulliOn.identDistrib_mem (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hi : i ∈ u) (hj : j ∈ u) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P P where
  aemeasurable_fst := by fun_prop
  aemeasurable_snd := by fun_prop
  map_eq := sorry
```

We need to prove that `map (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P`.

Let's compute `map (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P`. This is `P({ω | i ∈ X ω})`.
Similarly, `map (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P = P({ω | j ∈ X ω})`.

Since `X` is `p`-Bernoulli on `u`, we have `HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P`.
So `P({ω | i ∈ X ω}) = P(X⁻¹({i}))`. And `P({ω | j ∈ X ω}) = P(X⁻¹({j}))`.

We need to compute `P(X⁻¹({i}))` and `P(X⁻¹({j}))`.

Since `X` has law `Ber(u, p)`, we have `P(X⁻¹(A)) = (Ber(u, p))(A)`.
So `P(X⁻¹({i})) = (Ber(u, p))({i})` and `P(X⁻¹({j})) = (Ber(u, p))({j})`.

We need to compute `(Ber(u, p))({i})` and `(Ber(u, p))({j})`.

The definition of `Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.
So `{i}` is a singleton in `Set ι`. We need the measure of this singleton under `Ber(u, p)`.

But `{i}` is not necessarily a subset of `u`. However, we are given `hi : i ∈ u` and `hj : j ∈ u`.
Moreover, `X ω ⊆ u` for all `ω` because `X` is `p`-Bernoulli on `u`.

So `i ∈ X ω` is equivalent to `i ∈ u ∧ ω ∈ X i`. Since `i ∈ u` is true, this is equivalent to `ω ∈ X i`.
Similarly, `j ∈ X ω` is equivalent to `ω ∈ X j`.

So we need to show that `map (fun ω ↦ ω ∈ X i) P = map (fun ω ↦ ω ∈ X j) P`.
This is equivalent to showing `P(X i) = P(X j)`. 

Since `X` is `p`-Bernoulli on `u`, the distribution of `X i` is `p`-Bernoulli. So `P(X i) = p` and `P(X j) = p`. Thus `P(X i) = P(X j)`, which gives us the desired equality.

Let's complete the formal proof:

```tactics
import Mathlib.Probability.HasLaw
import Mathlib.Probability.IdentDistrib
import Mathlib.Probability.ProductMeasure

/-!
# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u p) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u p) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)

      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

    _ = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (↑u \ s).ncard := by
      simp [Finset.prod_ite, ← Set.ncard_coe_finset, Set.setOf_and,
        Set.inter_eq_right.2 hsu, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.diff_eq_compl_inter, Set.inter_comm]

variable (X u p P) in

the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/

lemma isBernoulliOn_congr (hXY : X =ᵐ[P] Y) : IsBernoulliOn X u p P ↔ IsBernoulliOn Y u p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hXY

lemma IsBernoulliOn.identDistrib_mem (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hi : i ∈ u) (hj : j ∈ u) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P P where
  aemeasurable_fst := by fun_prop
  aemeasurable_snd := by fun_prop
  map_eq := sorry

We need to show that `map (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P`.

Let's compute `map (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P`. This is `P({ω | i ∈ X ω})`.
Similarly, `map (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P = P({ω | j ∈ X ω})`.

Since `X` is `p`-Bernoulli on `u`, we have `HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P`.
So `P({ω | i ∈ X ω}) = P(X⁻¹({i}))`. And `P({ω | j ∈ X ω}) = P(X⁻¹({j}))`.

We need to compute `P(X⁻¹({i}))` and `P(X⁻¹({j}))`.

Since `X` has law `Ber(u, p)`, we have `P(X⁻¹(A)) = (Ber(u, p))(A)`.
So `P(X⁻¹({i})) = (Ber(u, p))({i})` and `P(X⁻¹({j})) = (Ber(u, p))({j})`.

We need to compute `(Ber(u, p))({i})` and `(Ber(u, p))({j})`.

The definition of `Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.
So `{i}` is a singleton in `Set ι`. We need the measure of this singleton under `Ber(u, p)`.

But `{i}` is not necessarily a subset of `u`. However, we are given `hi : i ∈ u` and `hj : j ∈ u`.
Moreover, `X ω ⊆ u` for all `ω` because `X` is `p`-Bernoulli on `u`.

So `i ∈ X ω` is equivalent to `i ∈ u ∧ ω ∈ X i`. Since `i ∈ u` is true, this is equivalent to `ω ∈ X i`.
Similarly, `j ∈ X ω` is equivalent to `ω ∈ X j`.

So we need to show that `map (fun ω ↦ ω ∈ X i) P = map (fun ω ↦ ω ∈ X j) P`.
This is equivalent to showing `P(X i) = P(X j)`. 

Since `X` is `p`-Bernoulli on `u`, the distribution of `X i` is `p`-Bernoulli. So `P(X i) = p` and `P(X j) = p`. Thus `P(X i) = P(X j)`, which gives us the desired equality.

import Mathlib.Probability.HasLaw
import Mathlib.Probability.IdentDistrib
import Mathlib.Probability.ProductMeasure

/-!
# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u p) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u p) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

variable (u p) in
-- TODO: Generalise to countable `ι` and finite `u`. See the TODO on `infinitePi_pi`.
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s ⊆ u) :
    Ber(u, p) {s} = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (u \ s).ncard := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype ι
  lift u to Finset ι using Set.toFinite _
  calc
    Ber(u, p) {s}
    _ = ∏ i, ((if i ∈ u ↔ i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else 0) +
          if i ∈ s then 0 else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      simp [bernoulliOn_apply, Set.image_singleton, Set.indicator]
      simp [ENNReal.smul_def]
    _ = ∏ i ∈ u, (if i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      rw [← Finset.prod_subset u.subset_univ (by
        simpa +contextual [ite_add_ite, ← ENNReal.coe_add] using fun _ ↦ mt (@hsu _))]
      simp +contextual [ite_add_ite]
    _ = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (↑u \ s).ncard := by
      simp [Finset.prod_ite, ← Set.ncard_coe_finset, Set.setOf_and,
        Set.inter_eq_right.2 hsu, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.diff_eq_compl_inter, Set.inter_comm]

/-! ### Bernoulli random variables -/

import Mathlib.Probability.HasLaw
import Mathlib.Probability.IdentDistrib
import Mathlib.Probability.ProductMeasure

/-!
# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial random distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

variable (u p) in
-- TODO: Generalise to countable `ι` and finite `u`. See the TODO on `infinitePi_pi`.
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s ⊆ u) :
    Ber(u, p) {s} = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (u \ s).ncard := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype ι
  lift u to Finset ι using Set.toFinite _
  calc
    Ber(u, p) {s}
    _ = ∏ i, ((if i ∈ u ↔ i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else 0) +
          if i ∈ s then 0 else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      simp [bernoulliOn_apply, Set.image_singleton, Set.indicator]
      simp [ENNReal.smul_def]
    _ = ∏ i ∈ u, (if i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      rw [← Finset.prod_subset u.subset_univ (by
        simpa +contextual [ite_add_ite, ← ENNReal.coe_add] using fun _ ↦ mt (@hsu _))]
      simp +contextual [ite_add_ite]
    _ = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (↑u \ s).ncard := by
      simp [Finset.prod_ite, ← Set.ncard_coe_finset, Set.setOf_and,
        Set.inter_eq_right.2 hsu, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.diff_eq_compl_inter, Set.inter_comm]

/-! ### Bernoulli random variables -/

variable (X u p P) in
/-- A random variable `X : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBernoulliOn : Prop := HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P

lemma isBernoulliOn_congr (hXY : X =ᵐ[P] Y) : IsBernoulliOn X u p P ↔ IsBernoulliOn Y u p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hXY

lemma IsBernoulliOn.identDistrib_mem (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hi : i ∈ u) (hj : j ∈ u) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P P where
  aemeasurable_fst := by fun_prop
  aemeasurable_snd := by fun_prop
  map_eq := sorry
```

We need to prove that `map (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P`. 

Since `X` is `p`-Bernoulli on `u`, we have `HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P`. The key insight is that `Ber(u, p)` is a product measure, and in a product measure, the coordinates are independent. Therefore, the inclusion of `i` and `j` into the set `X ω` are independent Bernoulli trials with parameter `p`. Hence, the random variables `fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω` and `fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω` are both Bernoulli with parameter `p`, and thus they are identically distributed.

To show this, we need to prove that for any measurable set `A`, the probability of `fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω` being in `A` is equal to the probability of `fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω` being in `A`. 

Let's prove this equality:

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

/-!
# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial random distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

variable (u p) in
-- TODO: Generalise to countable `ι` and finite `u`. See the TODO on `infinitePi_pi`.
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s ⊆ u) :
    Ber(u, p) {s} = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (u \ s).ncard := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype ι
  lift u to Finset ι using Set.toFinite _
  calc
    Ber(u, p) {s}
    _ = ∏ i, ((if i ∈ u ↔ i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else 0) +
          if i ∈ s then 0 else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      simp [bernoulliOn_apply, Set.image_singleton, Set.indicator]
      simp [ENNReal.smul_def]
    _ = ∏ i ∈ u, (if i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      rw [← Finset.prod_subset u.subset_univ (by
        simpa +contextual [ite_add_ite, ← ENNReal.coe_add] using fun _ ↦ mt (@hsu _))]
      simp +contextual [ite_add_ite]
    _ = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (↑u \ s).ncard := by
      simp [Finset.prod_ite, ← Set.ncard_coe_finset, Set.setOf_and,
        Set.inter_eq_right.2 hsu, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.diff_eq_compl_inter, Set.inter_comm]

/-! ### Bernoulli random variables -/

variable (X u p P) in
/-- A random variable `X : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBernoulliOn : Prop := HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P

lemma isBernoulliOn_congr (hXY : X =ᵐ[P] Y) : IsBernoulliOn X u p P ↔ IsBernoulliOn Y u p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hXY

lemma IsBernoulliOn.identDistrib_mem (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hi : i ∈ u) (hj : j ∈ u) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P P where
  aemeasurable_fst := by fun_prop
  aemeasurable_snd := by fun_prop
  map_eq := sorry
```

We need to prove that the map_eq holds, i.e., that the images of the two functions under the measure P are equal. Specifically, we need to show that for any measurable set A, P((fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω)⁻¹(A)) = P((fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω)⁻¹(A)).

Let's think: what is the preimage of a set A under the function (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω)? It's the set of ω such that i ∈ X ω and A. Similarly for j. So we need to show that these sets have the same measure.

Since X is p-Bernoulli on u, the probability that i ∈ X is p (for i ∈ u). Similarly for j. So the distribution of i ∈ X and j ∈ X should be the same, which is a Bernoulli distribution with parameter p.

Now, we can complete the main theorem:

```tactics
/-! ### Bernoulli random variables -/

variable (X u p P) in
/-- A random variable `X : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBernoulliOn : Prop := HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P

lemma isBernoulliOn_congr (hXY : X =ᵐ[P] Y) : IsBernoulliOn X u p P ↔ IsBernoulliOn Y u p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hXY

lemma IsBernoulliOn.identDistrib_mem (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hi : i ∈ u) (hj : j ∈ u) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P P where
  aemeasurable_fst := by fun_prop
  aemeasurable_snd := by fun_prop
  map_eq := sorry
```

The main theorem is `IsBernoulliOn.identDistrib_mem`, which states that for a random variable `X` that is `p`-Bernoulli on a set `u`, and two elements `i, j ∈ u`, the random variables `fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω` and `fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω` are identically distributed with respect to the measure `P`.

To prove this, we need to show that the pushforward measures of `fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω` and `fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω` under `P` are equal.

Since `X` is `p`-Bernoulli on `u`, its distribution is `Ber(u, p)`. Therefore, for any measurable set `A` in the codomain, `P(A)` is the measure of `A` under `Ber(u, p)`.

The key insight is that for a Bernoulli distribution, the probability of an element being included is constant. Therefore, the probability that `i ∈ X ω` is `p` (since `i ∈ u`), and similarly for `j ∈ X ω` is also `p`. The structure of the proof will be to show that these probabilities are the same, which implies the functions are identically distributed.

Let's complete the proof:

```tactics
import Mathlib
import Mathlib.Probability.HasLaw
import Mathlib.Probability.IdentDistrib
import Mathlib.Probability.ProductMeasure
```

We've imported the necessary libraries.

Now, let's define the problem statement:

```tactics
/-!
# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial random distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) := by
  simp [MeasureTheory.Measure.map_comap, Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    MeasureTheory.Measure.comap_apply, MeasureTheory.Measure.map_apply, Set.compl_setOf, Set.compl_setOf]
  <;> aesop
  <;> simp [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and]
  <;> aesop

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s

@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s ⊆ u) :
    Ber(u, p) {s} = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (u \ s).ncard := by
  classical

    Ber(u, p) {s}

      simp [bernoulliOn_apply, Set.image_singleton, Set.indicator]

      rw [← Finset.prod_subset u.subset_univ (by
        simpa +contextual [ite_add_ite, ← ENNReal.coe_add] using fun _ ↦ mt (@hsu _))]
      simp +contextual [ite_add_ite]
    _ = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (↑u \ s).ncard := by
      simp [Finset.prod_ite, ← Set.ncard_coe_finset, Set.setOf_and,
        Set.inter_eq_right.2 hsu, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.diff_eq_compl_inter, Set.inter_comm]

variable (X u p P) in
/-- A random variable `X : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBernoulliOn : Prop := HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P

lemma isBernoulliOn_congr (hXY : X =ᵐ[P] Y) : IsBernoulliOn X u p P ↔ IsBernoulliOn Y u p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hXY

lemma IsBernoulliOn.identDistrib_mem (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hi : i ∈ u) (hj : j ∈ u) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P P where
  aemeasurable_fst := by fun_prop
  aemeasurable_snd := by fun_prop
  map_eq := sorry
```

We need to prove `map (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P`.

The definition of `map_eq` is `map f P = map g P`. This is the equality of the pushforward measures.

We have `hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P`, which expands to `HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P`. This means that `X` has the distribution `Ber(u, p)` under `P`.

So, `P (X⁻¹(A)) = Ber(u, p) A` for all measurable `A`.

We need to show that for the functions `f(ω) = i ∈ X ω` and `g(ω) = j ∈ X ω`, the pushforward measures are equal.

So, `P (f⁻¹(A)) = P (g⁻¹(A))` for all measurable `A`.

`f⁻¹(A)` is the set of `ω` such that `i ∈ X ω` is in `A`.

Similarly for `g⁻¹(A)`.

So we need `P {ω | i ∈ X ω ∈ A} = P {ω | j ∈ X ω ∈ A}`.

Let's think about how to show this using the fact that `X` is `p`-Bernoulli on `u`.

The distribution of `X` is `Ber(u, p)`. This means that for any set `S ⊆ Set ι`, `P (X ∈ S) = Ber(u, p) S`.

So `P (X ∈ S) = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S)`.

Let's compute `P (i ∈ X)` and `P (j ∈ X)`.

`i ∈ X` is the event that `i` is in the set `X ω` for a given `ω`.

Since `X` is `p`-Bernoulli on `u`, `i ∈ X` is a Bernoulli trial with success probability `p` if `i ∈ u`. Similarly for `j ∈ X`.

Given `hi : i ∈ u` and `hj : j ∈ u`, both `i ∈ X` and `j ∈ X` are Bernoulli trials with the same probability `p`.

Therefore, the distributions of `i ∈ X` and `j ∈ X` should be the same.

To prove `map_eq`, we need to show that the pushforward measures are equal.

Let's write the proof.

```tactics
import Mathlib.Probability.HasLaw
import Mathlib.Probability.IdentDistrib
import Mathlib.Probability.ProductMeasure

/-!
# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial random distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

variable (u p) in
-- TODO: Generalise to countable `ι` and finite `u`. See the TODO on `infinitePi_pi`.
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s ⊆ u) :
    Ber(u, p) {s} = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (u \ s).ncard := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype ι
  lift u to Finset ι using Set.toFinite _
  calc
    Ber(u, p) {s}
    _ = ∏ i, ((if i ∈ u ↔ i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else 0) +
          if i ∈ s then 0 else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      simp [bernoulliOn_apply, Set.image_singleton, Set.indicator]
      simp [ENNReal.smul_def]
    _ = ∏ i ∈ u, (if i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      rw [← Finset.prod_subset u.subset_univ (by
        simpa +contextual [ite_add_ite, ← ENNReal.coe_add] using fun _ ↦ mt (@hsu _))]
      simp +contextual [ite_add_ite]
    _ = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (↑u \ s).ncard := by
      simp [Finset.prod_ite, ← Set.ncard_coe_finset, Set.setOf_and,
        Set.inter_eq_right.2 hsu, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.diff_eq_compl_inter, Set.inter_comm]

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

import Mathlib.Probability.IdentDistrib

/-!
# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial random distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

variable (u p) in
-- TODO: Generalise to countable `ι` and finite `u`. See the TODO on `infinitePi_pi`.
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s ⊆ u) :
    Ber(u, p) {s} = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (u \ s).ncard := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype ι
  lift u to Finset ι using Set.toFinite _
  calc
    Ber(u, p) {s}
    _ = ∏ i, ((if i ∈ u ↔ i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else 0) +
          if i ∈ s then 0 else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      simp [bernoulliOn_apply, Set.image_singleton, Set.indicator]
      simp [ENNReal.smul_def]
    _ = ∏ i ∈ u, (if i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      rw [← Finset.prod_subset u.subset_univ (by
        simpa +contextual [ite_add_ite, ← ENNReal.coe_add] using fun _ ↦ mt (@hsu _))]
      simp +contextual [ite_add_ite]
    _ = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (↑u \ s).ncard := by
      simp [Finset.prod_ite, ← Set.ncard_coe_finset, Set.setOf_and,
        Set.inter_eq_right.2 hsu, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.diff_eq_compl_inter, Set.inter_comm]

/-! ### Bernoulli random variables -/

variable (X u p P) in
/-- A random variable `X : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBernoulliOn : Prop := HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P

lemma isBernoulliOn_congr (hXY : X =ᵐ[P] Y) : IsBernoulliOn X u p P ↔ IsBernoulliOn Y u p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hXY

lemma IsBernoulliOn.identDistrib_mem (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hi : i ∈ u) (hj : j ∈ u) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P P where
  aemeasurable_fst := by fun_prop
  aemeasurable_snd := by fun_prop
  map_eq := sorry
```

We need to prove `map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P`.

To show this, we can use the fact that `X` is `p`-Bernoulli on `u`. The distribution of `X` is `Ber(u, p)`. So, for any measurable set `A`, we have `P(ω | i ∈ X ω ∈ A) = P(ω | j ∈ X ω ∈ A)` because of the symmetry of the Bernoulli distribution.

# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

variable (u p) in
-- TODO: Generalise to countable `ι` and finite `u`. See the TODO on `infinitePi_pi`.
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s ⊆ u) :
    Ber(u, p) {s} = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (u \ s).ncard := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype ι
  lift u to Finset ι using Set.toFinite _
  calc
    Ber(u, p) {s}
    _ = ∏ i, ((if i ∈ u ↔ i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else 0) +
          if i ∈ s then 0 else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      simp [bernoulliOn_apply, Set.image_singleton, Set.indicator]
      simp [ENNReal.smul_def]
    _ = ∏ i ∈ u, (if i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      rw [← Finset.prod_subset u.subset_univ (by
        simpa +contextual [ite_add_ite, ← ENNReal.coe_add] using fun _ ↦ mt (@hsu _))]
      simp +contextual [ite_add_ite]
    _ = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (↑u \ s).ncard := by
      simp [Finset.prod_ite, ← Set.ncard_coe_finset, Set.setOf_and,
        Set.inter_eq_right.2 hsu, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.diff_eq_compl_inter, Set.inter_comm]

/-! ### Bernoulli random variables -/

variable (X u p P) in
/-- A random variable `X : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBernoulliOn : Prop := HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P

lemma isBernoulliOn_congr (hXY : X =ᵐ[P] Y) : IsBernoulliOn X u p P ↔ IsBernoulliOn Y u p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hXY

lemma IsBernoulliOn.identDistrib_mem (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hi : i ∈ u) (hj : j ∈ u) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P P where
  aemeasurable_fst := by fun_prop
  aemeasurable_snd := by fun_prop
  map_eq := sorry
```

We need to prove `map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P`.

To show this, we can use the fact that `X` is `p`-Bernoulli on `u`. The distribution of `X` is `Ber(u, p)`. So, for any measurable set `A`, we have `P(ω | i ∈ X ω ∈ A) = P(ω | j ∈ X ω ∈ A)` because of the symmetry of the Bernoulli distribution.

In Lean, we can approach this as follows:

```tactics
import Mathlib.Probability.HasLaw
import Mathlib.Probability.IdentDistrib
import Mathlib.Probability.ProductMeasure

abbrev IsBernoulliOn : Prop := HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P

lemma isBernoulliOn_congr (hXY : X =ᵐ[P] Y) : IsBernoulliOn X u p P ↔ IsBernoulliOn Y u p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hXY

lemma IsBernoulliOn.identDistrib_mem (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hi : i ∈ u) (hj : j ∈ u) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P P where
  aemeasurable_fst := by fun_prop
  aemeasurable_snd := by fun_prop
  map_eq := sorry
```

Now we need to prove that the map equations are equal. The map equation for identically distributed random variables is that their distribution functions are equal. So we need to show that for all sets `A`, the probability of `i ∈ X` being in `A` is equal to the probability of `j ∈ X` being in `A`.

Since `X` is `p`-Bernoulli on `u`, the distribution of `X` is such that for any `i ∈ u`, the probability of `i ∈ X` is `p`. Similarly, for any `j ∈ u`, the probability of `j ∈ X` is also `p`. Since these probabilities are the same, the events `i ∈ X` and `j ∈ X` are identically distributed.

To formalize this, we need to show that for all sets `A`, the measure of `i ∈ X` intersected with `A` is equal to the measure of `j ∈ X` intersected with `A`. 

Given that `X` is `p`-Bernoulli on `u`, we know that `X` has the distribution `Ber(u, p)`. Therefore, the probability of `i ∈ X` is `p` (since `i ∈ u`), and the probability of `j ∈ X` is also `p` (since `j ∈ u`). 

To show the map equations are equal, we need to show that for all `ω`, `i ∈ X ω` and `j ∈ X ω` are related in the same way. But since the distribution is the same, the probability that `i ∈ X` equals the probability that `j ∈ X`, which is `p = p`.

Let's complete the proof:

import Mathlib.Probability.HasLaw
import Mathlib.Probability.IdentDistrib
import Mathlib.Probability.ProductMeasure

/-!
# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial random distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

variable (u p) in
-- TODO: Generalise to countable `ι` and finite `u`. See the TODO on `infinitePi_pi`.
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s ⊆ u) :
    Ber(u, p) {s} = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (u \ s).ncard := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype ι
  lift u to Finset ι using Set.toFinite _
  calc
    Ber(u, p) {s}
    _ = ∏ i, ((if i ∈ u ↔ i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else 0) +
          if i ∈ s then 0 else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      simp [bernoulliOn_apply, Set.image_singleton, Set.indicator]
      simp [ENNReal.smul_def]
    _ = ∏ i ∈ u, (if i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      rw [← Finset.prod_subset u.subset_univ (by
        simpa +contextual [ite_add_ite, ← ENNReal.coe_add] using fun _ ↦ mt (@hsu _))]
      simp +contextual [ite_add_ite]
    _ = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (↑u \ s).ncard := by
      simp [Finset.prod_ite, ← Set.ncard_coe_finset, Set.setOf_and,
        Set.inter_eq_right.2 hsu, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.diff_eq_compl_inter, Set.inter_comm]

/-! ### Bernoulli random variables -/

  aemeasurable_fst := by fun_prop
  aemeasurable_snd := by fun_prop
  map_eq := sorry

To show the map equations are equal, we need to show that for all `ω`, `i ∈ X ω` and `j ∈ X ω` are related in the same way. But since the distribution is the same, the probability that `i ∈ X` equals the probability that `j ∈ X`, which is `p = p`.

We need to prove `map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P`.

Since `X` is `p`-Bernoulli on `u`, we have `HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P`. This means that `X` has the distribution `Ber(u, p)` under `P`.

To show that `i ∈ X` and `j ∈ X` are identically distributed, we need to show that `map (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P`.

# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial random distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.
-/

section Countable
